name: Grade 6 Mathematics — Semester 2
description: An 18-week Grade 6 pathway for rational numbers, geometry, data distributions, and modeling. Learning advances through inquiry, practice, authentic application, revision, and communication.
learningGoals: Develop grade 6 knowledge and transferable skills in rational numbers, geometry, data distributions, and modeling.
Use appropriate evidence, vocabulary, tools, and methods to explain reasoning.
Plan, revise, collaborate responsibly, and communicate a well-supported conclusion.

lessons.title: Launch inquiry: rational numbers, geometry, data distributions, and modeling
lessons.objective: I can develop a focused question and gather relevant evidence about rational numbers, geometry, data distributions, and modeling and explain my reasoning with appropriate evidence.

units.title: Investigate: rational numbers, geometry, data distributions, and modeling
units.essentialQuestion: What question and evidence can guide our study of rational numbers, geometry, data distributions, and modeling?
outcomes: I can develop a focused question and gather relevant evidence about rational numbers, geometry, data distributions, and modeling.
I can use appropriate disciplinary vocabulary, evidence, and tools to communicate my reasoning.

activities.title: Question and evidence
activities.learnerAction: The learner develop a focused question and gather relevant evidence about rational numbers, geometry, data distributions, and modeling, documents evidence in an age-appropriate format, and follows applicable safety, ethics, and collaboration expectations.

successCriteria: The learner completes a grade 6 task connected to rational numbers, geometry, data distributions, and modeling.
The learner uses relevant evidence, vocabulary, or a model to explain reasoning.
The learner revises, seeks feedback, or identifies a specific next step.
